status_detail: Large-scale green hydrogen project in Magallanes, southern Chile, producing e-methanol for European markets using Spanish technology partners. Leverages world-class wind resources. 1 GW+ electrolysis planned. Export-focused.

data_management.narrative_summary: H2 Magallanes is a large-scale green hydrogen project in the Magallanes region of southern Chile, one of the world's best wind resource areas. The project is a partnership between Spanish and Chilean developers, targeting over 1 GW of electrolysis capacity producing green hydrogen that will be converted to e-methanol for export to European and Asian markets. The project benefits from Chile's National Green Hydrogen Strategy, which targets the country becoming one of the world's lowest-cost green hydrogen producers by 2030. With wind capacity factors exceeding 50% in the Magallanes region, the project could achieve some of the lowest green hydrogen production costs globally.

power.grid_connection_detail: Off-grid dedicated wind farm in Magallanes region

offtake.offtaker_name: European and Asian shipping fuel markets (e-methanol)

sources.title: H2 Magallanes — Chile green hydrogen project for e-methanol export
sources.author: Chilean Hydrogen Association / IEA
